---
title: "creative-data-visualization"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Load packages and create a toy dataset
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

# originally ran this with my data and worked!
#creating a toy dataset so I can share this without my actual data
set.seed(123)  # ensures reproducibility

sites <- paste0("S", sprintf("%02d", 1:12)) #creates 12 sites
species <- c("Oyster", "Crab", "Fish", "Shrimp", "Clam",
             "Worm", "Snail", "Jellyfish", "Ray", "Shark") #creates 10 possible species

toy_data <- expand.grid(site = sites,
  species = species) %>%
  mutate(
    present = rbinom(n(), 1, prob = 0.7),   # 70% chance species occurs
    abundance = ifelse(present == 1,
                        sample(1:50, n(), replace = TRUE), #abundance of each species can be up to 50 individuals
                        0)) %>%
  select(-present)

head(toy_data)
```

Inspiration
I wanted to create some form of abstract art to display oyster reef assemblages. I thought it would be fun to mimic this paint bubble style of art!
```{r}
#| out-width: ###px
knitr::include_graphics("image/bubble art.jpg")
```

Wrangle Data
```{r}
set.seed(42)  
# ensures your random pattern is reproducible (important for grading)

art_cloud <- toy_data %>%
  group_by(site, species) %>%  
  # Split data by site and species

  group_modify(~{

    n <- .x$abundance[1]  
    # Number of points to generate = abundance

    if (n == 0) return(tibble())  
    # Skip groups with zero abundance

    tibble(
      x = rnorm(n, runif(1, -3, 3), runif(1, 0.2, 1.2)),  
      # Random x positions around a random center

      y = rnorm(n, runif(1, -3, 3), runif(1, 0.2, 1.2))   
      # Random y positions around a random center
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()  
  # Remove grouping for normal data use
```

Plot data as abstract art!
```{r}
#specifies colors of species
species_colors <- hue_pal(l = 70, c = 40)(length(unique(art_cloud$species)))

#creates a column for point size
art_cloud$size <- runif(nrow(art_cloud), 0.8, 2)

ggplot(art_cloud, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(color = species, size = size),
    alpha = 0.14
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ site) +
  scale_color_manual(values = species_colors) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```
Each individual within a site's assemblage is an individual circle. Each color represents a species. Size does not represent anything, rather it is used for aesthetic variation to make it more visually interesting as an art piece.
